3.why: Everything pushed goes here first — it is the only stack push ever touches.
3.invariant: The inbox holds the NEWEST elements, newest on top.

4.why: The front of the queue will live on top of this stack once it is loaded.
4.invariant: The outbox holds the OLDEST elements, oldest on top.

5.happening: Create the results rail — one entry per operation.
5.why: LeetCode prints a value for every call, including null for push.
5.invariant: results always has one entry per operation replayed so far.

6.happening: Start at the first operation in the sequence.
6.why: We replay the calls in the exact order LeetCode makes them.
6.invariant: op_index is the operation about to run.

7.happening: Declare the holder for the operation name.
7.why: Each pass reads one name into it.
7.invariant: current_op names the operation being executed.

8.why: Counting transfers is how you see the amortized O(1) claim is real.

9.why: Each call is executed in sequence against the same two stacks.
9.invariant: Operations before index {op_index} are done and recorded.

9.why: The sequence is exhausted, so the results list is complete.
9.invariant: results holds one entry per operation.

10.why: The name decides which of the four branches runs.
10.invariant: Exactly one operation runs per pass.

11.happening: It's a push — value {values[op_index]} is going onto the inbox.
11.why: Pushes are cheap: drop it on top and you're done.

11.happening: Not a push — skip this branch.
11.why: Only one branch matches the operation name.
11.invariant: The stacks are unchanged by a branch that doesn't match.

12.happening: Drop {values[op_index]} on top of the inbox.
12.why: New arrivals always go to the back of the queue, which is the inbox's top.

13.why: Keeping a slot per operation keeps results aligned with the call list.
13.invariant: results stays the same length as the operations replayed.

14.happening: It's a {current_op} — we need the FRONT of the queue.
14.why: The front is the oldest element, which is buried at the BOTTOM of the inbox.
14.invariant: The front is only reachable from the top of the outbox.

14.happening: Not a pop or peek — no outbox work needed.
14.why: Only these two operations read the front of the queue.
14.invariant: The outbox is untouched by push and empty.

15.happening: The outbox is empty — time to tip the inbox over.
15.why: This reversal is the whole trick, and it only happens when the outbox has run dry.
15.invariant: A transfer happens only when the outbox is empty.

15.happening: The outbox still holds {len(out_stack)} element(s) — no transfer needed.
15.why: Refilling now would put newer elements ON TOP of older ones and break FIFO order.
15.invariant: Never transfer while the outbox is non-empty.

16.happening: {len(in_stack)} element(s) still in the inbox to move.
16.why: The whole inbox is tipped over in one go.
16.invariant: Each element crosses between stacks at most once.

16.happening: The inbox is empty — the transfer is complete.
16.why: Everything now sits in the outbox in reversed (oldest-first) order.
16.invariant: After a transfer the inbox is empty and the outbox is loaded.

17.happening: Lift the inbox's top element and drop it on the outbox.
17.why: This is the reversal: the LAST element out of the inbox is the FIRST one that went in, so the oldest ends up on top of the outbox.
17.invariant: Popping from one stack and pushing to another reverses order.

18.why: Each element is transferred at most once in its lifetime, so the expensive transfer averages out to O(1) per operation.
18.invariant: moved never exceeds the number of elements ever pushed.

19.happening: It's a pop — remove the front of the queue.
19.why: The outbox is loaded, so the front is right on top.

19.happening: Not a pop — skip this branch.
19.why: Only one branch matches the operation name.
19.invariant: The stacks are unchanged by a branch that doesn't match.

20.happening: Pop {out_stack[len(out_stack) - 1]} off the outbox — that is the queue's front.
20.why: After the reversal, the outbox's top is the oldest element still queued.
20.invariant: The element removed is always the one that waited longest.

21.happening: It's a peek — look at the front without removing it.
21.why: peek answers 'who is next?' without consuming anything.

21.happening: Not a peek — skip this branch.
21.why: Only one branch matches the operation name.
21.invariant: The stacks are unchanged by a branch that doesn't match.

22.happening: Read {out_stack[len(out_stack) - 1]} off the top of the outbox, leaving it in place.
22.why: Reading the last element is a standard stack peek — no removal.
22.invariant: Both stacks look identical before and after a peek.

23.why: The caller wants to know whether anything is queued at all.

23.happening: Not an empty check — skip this branch.
23.why: Only one branch matches the operation name.
23.invariant: The stacks are unchanged by a branch that doesn't match.

24.happening: Both stacks are empty — the queue is empty (true).
24.why: An element could be waiting in EITHER stack, so both must be checked.
24.invariant: The queue is empty only when both stacks are.

24.happening: The queue is not empty — inbox holds {len(in_stack)}, outbox holds {len(out_stack)}.
24.why: Checking only one stack would wrongly report empty while the other still holds elements.
24.invariant: The queue is empty only when both stacks are.

25.happening: Move on to the next operation.
25.why: The sequence is replayed strictly in order.

26.happening: All {len(operations)} operations replayed — returning their results.
26.why: This list is exactly what LeetCode compares against.
26.invariant: results has one entry per operation, in call order.

init.happening: Creating two empty stacks and the results rail.
init.why: One stack takes arrivals, the other serves departures.

loop.happening: Replaying the operation sequence, or tipping the inbox into the outbox.
loop.why: Each call is executed in turn against the same pair of stacks.
loop.invariant: Operations run in the order they were issued.

check.happening: Deciding which operation this is, or whether the outbox needs refilling.
check.why: Each operation name selects exactly one branch.
check.invariant: No state changes on a check line.

update.happening: Pushing, transferring, or reading an element and recording the result.
update.why: These are the only lines that change the stacks.
update.invariant: Every operation appends exactly one entry to results.

move.happening: Advancing to the next operation in the sequence.
move.why: The replay marches forward one call at a time.

return.happening: Returning the result of every operation.
return.why: The whole sequence has been replayed.
